title: Updating from CentOS 8.0 to 8.1 broke all pods
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

After updating my CentOS 8.1 all pods have stopped working. Running `podman pod ps` hangs, attempting to stop existing pods hangs as well. The new `system reset` command also hangs.

Any information on how I can recover from this situation would be appreciated - like how can I manually remove these broken pods and recreate them?  Note I've also tried installing Podman 1.8 through the Kubic project, same issue.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Not sure what exactly needs to be done, but basically I have an existing pod structure on a CentOS server, and updating from 8.0 to 8.1 broke all pods.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.6.4
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

```
podman-1.6.4-2.module_el8.1.0+272+3e64ee36.x86_64
```

If it helps, here's the full output when running `podman pod ls` with debug log level:

```

comments.body:
Could you try
podman system renumber

Note to self: we can detect and prevent these deadlocks before they happen
by checking lock numbers before locking. Will work up a PR to this effect
soon.

On Sun, Feb 23, 2020, 09:44 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> Could you try
> podman system renumber
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/5303?email_source=notifications&email_token=AB3AOCGJEW6DREJ2UHXTA23REKDVVA5CNFSM4KZZBCL2YY3PNVWWK3TUL52HS4DFVREXG43VMVBW63LNMVXHJKTDN5WW2ZLOORPWSZGOEMV5PQQ#issuecomment-590075842>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCF3ADB4MFDUMQ2R5WDREKDVVANCNFSM4KZZBCLQ>
> .
>

> Could you try
> podman system renumber

Yes, that worked, thank you!
Not sure if you want to keep this issue open for the deadlock detection, but it's good to have this workaround.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
